records.ety: [OE. tow, AS. tow, akin to OD. touw, Icel. tō a tuft of wool for spinning; cf. E. taw, v. t.]

defs: The coarse and broken part of flax or hemp, separated from the finer part by the hatchel or swingle.

records.ety: [OE. towen, toen; akin to OFries. toga to pull about, OHG. zogōn, Icel. toga, AS. tohline a towline, and AS. ten to draw, p. p. getogen. See Tug.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Towed (tōd); p. pr. & vb. n. Towing.]

defs: To draw or pull through the water, as a vessel of any kind, by means of a rope.

records.ety: [Cf. Icel. taug a rope, from the same root as E. tow, v. t.]

defs: A rope by which anything is towed; a towline, or towrope.
The act of towing, or the state of being towed; -- chiefly used in the phrase, to take in tow, that is to tow.
That which is towed, or drawn by a towline, as a barge, raft, collection of boats, ect.
